incorrects.description: Student pressed Column + wrong number of times on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. We need 2 columns. Check how many you added.

incorrects.description: Student pressed Column + wrong number of times on second attempt

params.description: Column counter pulses to highlight current value.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. You need exactly 2 columns. Look at the counter and press + until it shows 2.

incorrects.description: Student pressed Column + wrong number of times three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System presses Column + twice, counter changes 0 to 1 to 2.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I press Column + two times. Now we have 2 empty columns. The answer is 2 columns.

incorrects.description: Student pressed Items per Column + wrong number of times on first attempt

steps.text: Almost. We need 5 dots in each column. Count how many you added.

incorrects.description: Student pressed Items per Column + wrong number of times on second attempt

params.description: Items per Column counter pulses to highlight current value.

steps.text: You're working on it. Here's what helps: each press adds 1 dot to every column. Keep pressing until each column has 5 dots total.

incorrects.description: Student pressed Items per Column + wrong number of times three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System presses Items per Column + five times. Counter changes 0 to 1 to 2 to 3 to 4 to 5. Dots fill into both columns one at a time.
Complete 5×2 dot array visible. Each column has 5 dots, 10 dots total.

steps.text: This one takes a bit of thought, so let's work through it together. I press Items per Column + five times. Each press puts 1 dot in every column. After 5 presses, each column has 5 dots. The answer is 5 dots per column.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong equation on first attempt

steps.text: Let's look at this problem again. You built 2 columns. Each column has 5 dots.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong equation on second attempt

params.description: The 2 columns highlight briefly, then the 5 dots in each column highlight.

steps.text: Here's a hint: count the columns first. That's your first number. Then count how many dots are in one column. That's your second number.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong equation three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System places tiles: 2, then ×, then 5, then =, then 10. Each tile appears in sequence.
As equation builds, 2 columns highlight, then 5 dots in each column highlight, then all 10 dots highlight.

steps.text: Let me show you how to think about this. First, I count the columns: 2. Then I count the dots in one column: 5. The equation is 2 times 5 equals 10. That matches what we built.
